persona.name: Jordan the Freelance Brand Designer
persona.summary: Jordan is a solo Freelance Brand Designer in the design services industry who uses the tool to manage branding projects end-to-end — from browsing marketing templates and building brand kits to exporting assets and sharing deliverables with clients. They are highly time-conscious because they bill hourly, so any feature that saves 10 minutes per project is immediately valuable to them. Jordan's top unmet need is a white-labeled client share view that lets them deliver a professional, branded experience without exposing the underlying tool.

demographics.education_level: Design school or self-taught creative professional

goals: Find and customize marketing templates quickly to accelerate client deliverable production
Build and maintain reusable brand kits (colors, assets) for each client to ensure consistency
Share polished client-facing deliverables through a white-labeled view that hides tool branding
Collect and resolve client feedback efficiently via comment threading without back-and-forth email chains
pains: Client share view shows tool branding, undermining the professional image Jordan presents to clients
Template browsing requires multiple long sessions to find suitable options, indicating discoverability issues
Each project requires manually rebuilding brand settings since there is no efficient per-client brand kit management
Time spent on tool setup and asset management cuts into billable hours that directly affect income
motivations: Maximize billable efficiency — every 10 minutes saved per project is real monetary value for an hourly freelancer
Present a seamless, professional brand experience to clients that hides underlying tooling
Reduce project overhead through reusable templates and brand kits so more time goes to creative work
objections: Will not fully commit unless white-labeling is available for the client share view — a core professional need
Tool cost must be justified relative to the billable time it saves per project
Concerns that template library is not deep enough in marketing/branding categories to replace current workflow
channels: Freelancer communities (Reddit r/freelance, Independent Designers Slack)
YouTube design tutorials and tool walkthroughs
decision_triggers: White-labeled client share view is available or on near-term roadmap
Tool saves measurable time (10+ minutes) per project relative to current workflow
Brand kit supports multiple clients and is easily switchable between projects
Template library has sufficient depth in marketing and branding categories
sample_quotes: I bill clients hourly so anything that saves me 10 minutes per project is worth real money. Can your client share view be white-labeled?
I spend way too long hunting for a template that's close enough to customize — the library needs better filters.
If my client sees your logo when I share the deliverable, it breaks the illusion that this is my work.
I want to build a brand kit once per client and reuse it across every project without recreating it from scratch.

journey_stages.mindset: Browsing to see if the template and brand kit features can replace current fragmented workflow
Testing the core brand workflow: build a kit, export assets, share with a client
key_actions: client_share to test the share experience
content_preferences: Video walkthroughs of client share flow

journey_stages.mindset: Using the tool as part of an active client engagement, gathering feedback and iterating

source_evidence.claim: Freelance Brand Designer, solo, in design services industry
Browses marketing templates extensively to accelerate client deliverable production
Builds brand kits with color palettes for client consistency
Requests white-labeled client share view to present professional deliverables
Uses comment threading for client feedback collection
Client share view shows tool branding, undermining professional image
Template browsing requires multiple long sessions indicating discoverability issues
Time spent on tool setup cuts into billable hours that directly affect income
Motivated to maximize billable efficiency — every 10 minutes saved is real money
Motivated to present a seamless, professional brand experience to clients
Motivated to reduce project overhead through reusable templates and brand kits
Will not fully commit unless white-labeling is available for client share view
Tool cost must be justified relative to billable time saved per project
Concern that template library depth is insufficient in marketing/branding categories
Manually rebuilds brand settings per project — no efficient per-client brand kit management
Exports assets as part of final deliverable packaging for clients

twin_demo_reply: The client share view — no question. Every time I send a deliverable link, my client sees the tool logo instead of mine, and it immediately undermines the professional image I have spent years building. I bill hourly, so every workaround I patch together to hide the branding is eating into my margin. White-labeling should be a basic feature for freelancers, not a premium add-on I have to beg for.
